scenario: Trump extends ceasefire indefinitely (Apr 21, Pakistan request); Iranian govt 'seriously fractured'; US blockade remains; Iran: extension 'has no meaning'; Touska held; Brent ~$94 est. (-5.5%; last confirmed $95.42 TradingEconomics Apr 20); tail risk 33%; Iran retaliation vow unresolved

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat eases on ceasefire extension (Trump Apr 21) — partial relief from tail-risk premium. P&I cancellation and blockade still intact; Cape routing cost premium persists. Fertilizer supply disruption mechanism unchanged. Source: Barchart ZWK26 (est.).

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Biofuel correlation eases with Brent ~$94 on ceasefire extension. Indonesian ethanol demand trajectory remains elevated but reprices on extension relief. Fertilizer and freight disruption mechanism unchanged — blockade intact.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Bunker costs ease with Brent ~$94 on ceasefire extension. Indonesian and Vietnamese crush margins partially recover. Blockade intact — Cape routing costs remain elevated; structural supply chain disruption unresolved.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO eases on ceasefire extension; Brent crude correlation softens to ~$94. Indonesia B40 mandate provides structural floor. Fertilizer disruption for Q2 planting cycle partially offset by extension but blockade intact. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD.

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: ASEAN food security pressure eases modestly on ceasefire extension. Q2 planting season urea availability risk persists — blockade intact, Qafco force majeure unresolved. Extension provides diplomatic time but no supply chain resolution.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha eases with Brent ~$94 on ceasefire extension. Blockade remains — Gulf condensate interdiction persists; Qatar force majeure unresolved; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026 (The National). Jurong Island and Map Ta Phut feedstock pressure elevated but partially offset on extension relief.

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene follows naphtha lower on ceasefire extension. SABIC and QAPCO export bottlenecks persist — blockade intact; vessel interdiction not lifted. Agricultural film and irrigation pipe lead times remain extended. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol eases on ceasefire extension. US Iran oil sanctions waiver expiry (Bloomberg Apr 14) continues to tighten Iranian methanol enforcement. Qatar force majeure unresolved; blockade intact — freight disruption persists. China MTO complexes see slight feedstock relief. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea eases on ceasefire extension but remains severely elevated. Qafco force majeure persists; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; blockade intact — Gulf fertilizer export disruption unresolved. US waiver expiry continues tightening Iranian-origin enforcement. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP eases on ceasefire extension. Saudi Ma'aden shipping still disrupted; US Gulf supply stretched; blockade intact. Wet-season planting window in Philippines and Vietnam remains acute — extension without Hormuz reopening does not resolve supply chain.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight costs ease slightly on ceasefire extension. Less directly exposed to Hormuz than urea or DAP — freight remains main transmission. Cape rerouting costs persist with blockade intact; palm oil plantations in Malaysia and Indonesia face moderately elevated input costs.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI eases on ceasefire extension — Cape rerouting demand softens but blockade and P&I suspension persist. Normalization remains distant: no Hormuz reopening, no P&I restoration. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates ease on ceasefire extension but remain severely elevated — blockade intact, P&I suspension unresolved, no Hormuz reopening. Iran retaliation vow unresolved; tail (33%) would push rates to cycle highs. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: Container rates ease on ceasefire extension. Cape rerouting premium persists — blockade intact, P&I unresolved. Asia-Europe normalization requires Hormuz reopening + P&I restoration; extension provides neither. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (est.).
